target.title: fix(agents): filter unresolved model registry rows
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #66961.

- Filter generated `models.json` provider rows before write so model-bearing providers only survive when they have a usable execution contract: `baseUrl` plus usable auth, or an explicit runtime-accepted no-key auth mode such as `aws-sdk` / `oauth`.
- Keep resolved OAuth catalog rows only when the marker matches the provider, preserve supported local non-secret markers only on local/private endpoints, and drop stale remote/local/OAuth-looking markers.
- Improve the session/model-selection error for catalog-visible models blocked by `agents.defaults.models`.

## AI assistance disclosure

I used AI assistance for implementation, review, and proof collection. I reviewed and understand the affected path: generated `models.json` planning/merge, local auth marker resolution, model allowlist diagnostics, and the Gateway `sessions.patch` behavior.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: unresolved generated custom providers are excluded from `models.json`, runtime-accepted OAuth/aws-sdk and supported local-marker exceptions still work, and a catalog-visible but disallowed Codex model reports the allowlist fix.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, fake local `CODEX_API_KEY`, explicit loopback Gateway port/token.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
corepack pnpm exec tsx .artifacts/issue-66961-proof/run-proof.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ openclaw models list --all --provider codex --plain
codex/gpt-5.4

$ ensureOpenClawModelsJson(loadConfig({pin:false}))
{"agentDir":"$PROOF_DIR/state/agents/main/agent","wrote":true}

$ inspect generated models.json
{
  "unresolvedPresent": false,
  "oauthAuthPresent": true,
  "oauthAuthHasApiKey": false,
  "oauthLocalLookingMarkerPresent": false,
  "chutesApiKey": "oauth:chutes",
  "amazonBedrockPresent": false,
  "amazonBedrockHasApiKey": false,
  "responsesLocalPreserved": true,
  "ollamaDockerPreserved": true,
  "ollamaSidecarPreserved": true,
  "lmstudioLocalPreserved": true,
  "singleLabelCustomLocalMarkerPresent": false,
  "remoteLocalMarkerPresent": false,
  "codexModels": [
    "gpt-5.4"
  ]
}

$ openclaw gateway run --bind loopback --auth token --allow-unconfigured
gateway_ready: true

$ backend callGateway sessions.patch {key, model}
{
  "ok": false,
  "name": "GatewayClientRequestError",
  "message": "model not allowed: codex/gpt-5.4 (model is listed in the catalog, but not allowed by agents.defaults.models; add \"codex/gpt-5.4\" or \"codex/*\" to agents.defaults.models)",
  "gatewayCode": "INVALID_REQUEST"
}
```

Observed result after fix: unresolved token and non-env SecretRef providers are not written, missing-key `oauth` and blank `aws-sdk` auth stay keyless under the current runtime contract, matching `oauth:chutes` is preserved, local `custom-local`/`ollama-local`/`lmstudio-local` rows are preserved only on supported local endpoints, Ollama sidecar service-alias URLs survive refresh, unsafe single-label/remote local-marker rows are dropped, and `sessions.patch` reports the concrete allowlist change needed for `codex/gpt-5.4`.

What was not tested: real provider calls, real OAuth refresh, real AWS SDK credential resolution, and remote provider network requests. The proof uses fake local credentials and exercises admission/model-selection behavior only.

## Root Cause

Generated and merged `models.json` rows could keep model-bearing providers that were not executable by the runtime registry. A non-empty `apiKey` field was treated too broadly, so unresolved token auth, non-env SecretRef placeholders, stale OAuth markers, blank keys, and local no-auth markers attached to remote endpoints could survive into generated provider config.

The model registry requires model-bearing providers to have `baseUrl` and usable auth, except for explicit missing-key modes like `aws-sdk`. When invalid generated rows survived, they could poison catalog loading or produce confusing model-selection failures.

## Dependency contract

No external dependency contract changed.

Internal contracts checked:

- `src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts` validates model-bearing provider rows with `baseUrl` and `apiKey`/OAuth, and currently accepts missing-key `aws-sdk` and `oauth` auth modes.
- `src/agents/models-config.providers.secrets.ts` can emit matching OAuth markers such as `oauth:chutes` after OAuth-backed provider auth is resolved.
- `extensions/ollama/src/discovery-shared.ts` treats `ollama-local` as the documented synthetic local key for local Ollama endpoints.
- `extensions/lmstudio/openclaw.plugin.json` declares `lmstudio-local` as a bundled non-secret local marker.

## Regression Test Plan

- Generated write path drops unresolved token, stale OAuth-looking, non-env SecretRef, unsafe local-marker, and remote-local-marker providers.
- Generated write path preserves matching OAuth marker, missing-key `oauth`, `aws-sdk`, and supported local marker providers.
- Merge path drops stale invalid existing provider rows and preserves only self-contained existing rows.
- Runtime auth resolution normalizes supported local markers and rejects remote/non-local marker use.
- Gateway/session and cron model-selection tests cover the improved catalog-visible allowlist message.

## Security Impact

Positive: unresolved or stale credential markers are no longer persisted as executable model provider rows.

The local marker rule is intentionally conservative. It accepts known non-secret local markers only on loopback/private/local endpoints, IPv6 local ranges, known host-backed local aliases, or the documented Ollama service alias. It rejects OAuth marker syntax and arbitrary single-label OpenAI-compatible hosts.

## Human Verification

I reviewed the generated models path, auth marker predicate, model registry admission contract, model-selection diagnostics, focused tests, autoreview findings, `codex review` findings, and isolated Gateway proof output.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

Touched auth files match `.github/CODEOWNERS` entries for `@openclaw/openclaw-secops`:

- `/src/agents/*auth*.ts`
- `/src/agents/**/*auth*.ts`

Expected owner review: `@openclaw/openclaw-secops`.

## Changelog

No `CHANGELOG.md` edit. This is a contributor fix; release-note context is in this PR body.

## Review conversations

- Autoreview found an OAuth-marker over-admission issue for markers ending in `-local`; fixed by excluding colon marker syntaxes from local marker classification.
- Autoreview found arbitrary single-label host over-admission; fixed by narrowing the exception to the documented `ollama` service alias.
- ClawSweeper found a compatibility issue for existing missing-key `auth: "oauth"` rows; fixed by preserving the current runtime-accepted OAuth contract while still dropping stale OAuth marker strings.
- `codex review --disable image_generation --base upstream/main` found that matching OAuth markers must be preserved; fixed by accepting only `oauth:<providerId>` for the same provider.
- `codex review --disable image_generation --base upstream/main` found that existing local markers could override refreshed real local provider keys; fixed by letting the refreshed key win.
- Autoreview found that Ollama service-alias base URLs could be lost during refresh; fixed by using the same alias-aware local marker contract for base URL preservation.
- Final autoreview: accepted findings fixed. A repeated unused-import finding for `isLocalBaseUrl` was rejected after source search and `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/model-auth.ts` proved the import is used.
- Final `codex review --disable image_generation --base upstream/main`: clean, no actionable correctness issues.

## Risks

- The local-marker predicate still needs maintainer judgment for future plugin-owned non-secret markers. The current rule is conservative and driven by bundled local provider contracts.

## Behavior tradeoffs

- Matching `oauth:<providerId>` is treated as usable generated auth evidence for that provider; missing-key `auth: "oauth"` rows are preserved to match the current runtime contract; stale or mismatched OAuth marker strings are dropped.
- Arbitrary single-label OpenAI-compatible hosts are not treated as local no-auth endpoints. Users with non-standard hostnames can provide real credentials or use a supported local/private endpoint form.

## Scope boundaries

- Does not change `agents.defaults.models` allowlist semantics.
- Does not add runtime compatibility for non-env SecretRefs in generated `models.json`.
- Does not change the current missing-key OAuth runtime contract.
- Does not change provider discovery, provider calls, OAuth refresh, AWS SDK auth, or plugin registry behavior.
- Does not edit docs or changelog.

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts src/agents/models-config.skips-writing-models-json-no-env-token.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.model-formatting.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-resolve.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/models-config.uses-first-github-copilot-profile-env-tokens.test.ts
corepack pnpm tsgo:core && corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test
corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/model-auth-local.ts src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.ts src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts src/agents/models-config.merge.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.skips-writing-models-json-no-env-token.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.model-formatting.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/model-selection.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts
git diff --check
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/model-auth.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main
codex review --disable image_generation --base upstream/main
corepack pnpm exec tsx .artifacts/issue-66961-proof/run-proof.ts
```

target.title: fix(auto-reply): clear delivered pending final before abort
target.body:
## Summary

- Clear durable `pendingFinalDelivery` state immediately after a final reply is successfully delivered.
- Also clear `pendingFinalDeliveryIntentId` with the rest of the pending-final retry fields, matching the existing agent-command cleanup shape.
- Surface a post-delivery dispatch abort only after cleanup completes, so cancellation reporting is preserved without stranding session state.
- Preserve durable pending-final retry state when an accepted final dispatch later reports a failed final outcome.
- Add focused regression coverage plus a real-runtime proof script for the post-send abort window.

Fixes #89115.
Related: #89344.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs scripts/proof-89115-pending-final-delivery-abort.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/proof-89115-pending-final-delivery-abort.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/tsx scripts/proof-89115-pending-final-delivery-abort.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/proof-89115-pending-final-delivery-abort.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: In the final-dispatch success path, an abort that arrived after a successful final send but before `clearPendingFinalDeliveryAfterSuccess()` could leave `pendingFinalDelivery` and its durable retry fields set. That made the reply visible to the user while later inbound work could still be blocked by stale pending-final state. This update proves the post-send abort window, clears `pendingFinalDeliveryIntentId` with the rest of the durable pending-final fields, and preserves retry state when an accepted final dispatch later reports a failed final outcome.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS arm64 (`Darwin arm64`) with Node `v24.8.0`, at head `19703eb62ee3563907ee1039a867b3d60034e715`. The proof script uses production `dispatchReplyFromConfig()`, real `updateSessionStoreEntry()`/`loadSessionStore()` against an actual temporary `sessions.json`, a real empty plugin registry/hook runner, and a concrete dispatcher; it does not use Vitest mocks.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh

./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/proof-89115-pending-final-delivery-abort.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
```

Evidence after fix:


Observed result after fix: The proof script seeds a real `sessions.json` entry with `pendingFinalDelivery`, pending text, timestamps, attempt count, last error, delivery context, and `pendingFinalDeliveryIntentId`. The dispatcher accepts the final payload once, then aborts immediately in the post-send window. `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` returns through the existing aborted-dispatch path (`queuedFinal:false`) while preserving the accepted final count (`final:1`), and both `loadSessionStore()` and raw persisted JSON show no pending-final fields left afterward. The focused regression tests also cover the failed-final retry path, the intentional-cancellation cleanup path, and the existing internal source-reply cancellation path from the wider auto-reply dispatch shard.

What was not tested: A live external channel transport under production load. target.title: refactor(whatsapp): add inbound admission foundation
target.body:
## Summary

- add a public-safe WhatsApp inbound `admission` envelope for accepted `WebInboundMessage` callbacks
- build admission from the resolved ingress/access-control result and attach it in the real web inbox monitor path
- keep blocked access-control results admission-free while preserving the existing legacy access fields
- deprecate the superseded top-level admission fields (`from`, `conversationId`, `accountId`, `accessControlPassed`, `chatType`) as live aliases over `msg.admission`
- record the admission-field deprecation in the plugin compatibility registry and docs with the same `removeAfter` window as the prior inbound-context compatibility work

## Scope

This PR is an admission foundation plus compatibility bookkeeping. It does not migrate downstream identity/peer resolution, reply delivery, agent dispatch, or post-admission lifecycle handoff consumers to read `msg.admission` yet.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts src/plugins/compat/registry.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/message-aliases.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.behavior.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/plugins/compatibility.md extensions/whatsapp/api.ts extensions/whatsapp/runtime-api.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound-policy.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/access-control.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/admission.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/group-conversation.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/message-aliases.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/message-aliases.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/monitor.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/types.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.streams-inbound-messages.test-support.ts src/plugins/compat/registry.test.ts src/plugins/compat/registry.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm docs:list`

## Notes

- Accepted WhatsApp inbound access-control results now carry `admission`; blocked results intentionally do not.
- Admission contains redacted ingress/access projections, account facts, conversation facts, and sender facts without exposing raw allowlist material.
- Deprecated top-level admission fields remain available as getter/setter aliases during the compatibility window, so old and new callback code do not drift when either side is read or updated.
- `accessControlPassed` remains derived from `admission.ingress.decision === "allow"`; writing the legacy boolean does not fabricate a partial ingress graph.
- No live WhatsApp device/Testbox pass is claimed for this PR.

target.title: [AI-assisted] Preserve thread sessions across daily reset by default
target.body:
## Problem

Thread-scoped chat sessions currently inherit the global implicit daily reset policy when users do not configure `session.resetByType.thread`. For long-running Telegram/Slack/Discord topics this can rotate the OpenClaw session after the daily boundary even though the human-facing thread is still the same conversation. That breaks continuity before normal context-budget compaction has a chance to manage the history.

## Fix

Default implicit `thread` reset policy to `idle` with the existing default idle window (`0`, no inactivity expiry), while preserving all explicit configuration behavior:

- `session.reset` still applies when configured.
- `session.resetByType.thread` still overrides thread behavior.
- Direct/group sessions keep the existing implicit daily default.
- Legacy `session.idleMinutes` behavior is unchanged.

This lets long-running threads stay in the same session by default and rely on the existing compaction/context-budget path instead of being rotated daily.

## Validation

- `npm test -- --run src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`
  - 2 Vitest shards passed
  - 142 tests passed
- `npm run tsgo:test:src`
  - passed
- `git diff --check`
  - passed

`npm run check:test-types` could not run in this local environment because `pnpm` is not installed; I ran the src type gate directly with `npm run tsgo:test:src`.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A thread-scoped Telegram session that started before the daily reset boundary should remain the same OpenClaw session after the boundary when there is no explicit thread reset config. This preserves conversation continuity so the existing compaction/context-budget path can manage long-running threads.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch, run from a clean temporary session store under `/tmp` using the real `initSessionState` runtime path via `node_modules/.bin/tsx`. No mocks, no test runner, and no private production transcript data were used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Created a temporary `sessions.json`.
  2. Seeded `agent:main:telegram:group:-100123:topic:456` with `sessionId: "proof-thread-session"`.
  3. Set `sessionStartedAt`, `lastInteractionAt`, and `updatedAt` to 03:00.
  4. Invoked `initSessionState` at 05:00 with `Provider: "telegram"`, `Surface: "telegram"`, `ChatType: "group"`, and `MessageThreadId: 456`.
  5. Left `session.reset` and `session.resetByType.thread` unset.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the local runtime smoke command:
  ```json
  {"isNewSession":false,"sessionId":"proof-thread-session","reusedExisting":true}
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The thread session reused the existing `sessionId` after the 04:00 daily reset boundary. Before this change, the implicit daily reset policy would classify the same thread session as stale and rotate it to a new `sessionId`.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live Telegram bot conversation or the full CI matrix. I also did not add family-history retrieval APIs such as `includeFamily` / `resetAncestors`; that is a separate follow-up if maintainers want tool-visible history stitching.

target.title: feat: add before_route_inbound_message plugin hook for channel bridging
target.body:
## Summary

Implements the `before_route_inbound_message` plugin hook as proposed in #81061.

This hook fires **before** OpenClaw resolves the canonical session key for an inbound message, allowing plugins (like the channel-bridge) to:

- **Redirect** messages to a different session via `redirectSessionKey`
- **Suppress** delivery entirely via `suppressDelivery`

## Hook Signature

```ts
type PluginHookBeforeRouteInboundMessageEvent = {
  channel: string;
  accountId?: string;
  conversationId?: string;   // NativeChannelId or OriginatingTo
  parentConversationId?: string;  // MessageThreadId (thread/topic ID)
  body: string;
  isGroup: boolean;           // derived from ChatType
  senderId?: string;          // From field
  originalSessionKey: string;
};

type PluginHookBeforeRouteInboundMessageResult = {
  handled: true;
  redirectSessionKey?: string;
  suppressDelivery?: boolean;
};
```

## Integration Points

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/plugins/hook-types.ts` | Added to `PluginHookName` union, `PLUGIN_HOOK_NAMES` array, and `PluginHookHandlerMap` |
| `src/plugins/hook-message.types.ts` | New event/context/result type definitions |
| `src/plugins/hooks.ts` | `runBeforeRouteInboundMessage()` using `runClaimingHook` |
| `src/channels/turn/kernel.ts` | Hook fired in `runChannelTurn()` after `resolveTurn()` |

## Pattern

Uses the **claiming hook** pattern (`runClaimingHook`) — first handler returning `{ handled: true }` wins, matching `before_dispatch` and `reply_dispatch` semantics. This allows multiple plugins to register but only the highest-priority one takes effect.

## Why Here?

Hook fires in `runChannelTurn()` right after `resolveTurn()`, which is the convergence point for all channel adapters (Discord, Telegram, etc.). At this point:
- `ctxPayload.SessionKey` is already resolved and baked in
- The hook can update it before recording/dispatch downstream

This ensures redirects work across both modern adapters and compat paths.

## Unit Tests

Added 2 new tests in `src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts`:

```
PASS   src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts > channel turn kernel > redirects to a different session when before_route_inbound_message hook returns a redirect
PASS   src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts > channel turn kernel > suppresses delivery when before_route_inbound_message hook returns suppressDelivery
```

Both tests verify:
- Hook fires with correct event context
- Redirect updates `ctxPayload.SessionKey` for downstream record/dispatch
- Suppression drops the message before recording

## Proof: supplied

This PR adds a new plugin hook infrastructure with unit test coverage. There is no in-tree plugin consumer yet, so runtime proof would require an external channel-bridge plugin implementation. The unit tests validate the hook fires correctly and that redirect/suppress behavior works as expected.

Fixes: #81061

target.title: fix(agents): truncate tool results before overflow compaction
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: tool-heavy context overflows could wait for the full LLM auto-compaction timeout before trying the existing deterministic tool-result truncation recovery.
- Why it matters: small follow-up turns in long-lived Telegram sessions can appear hung for minutes even when tool-result truncation would be enough to recover.
- What changed: when an overflow attempt has oversized/aggregate tool-result pressure and no preflight recovery route is active, the run loop tries tool-result truncation before explicit overflow compaction; if truncation does not help, it falls back to the existing compaction path.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no config knobs, no compaction timeout changes, no changes to preflight/mid-turn recovery semantics.

AI-assisted: yes — implemented with an AI coding assistant and manually reviewed/verified locally.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #81182
- Related #53008
- Related #64962
- Related #78562
- Related #45503
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: overflow recovery in a tool-heavy Telegram direct session waited for auto-compaction to time out before truncating 281 tool results.
- Real environment tested: Docker-isolated OpenClaw runtime built from this PR checkout (`OpenClaw 2026.5.12-beta.1 (7ec0b4b)`) plus the original live OpenClaw Telegram direct-session before log, with private identifiers redacted in #81182. The Docker proof used a temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-proof-home`, synthetic tool-result payloads only, and no production Telegram token, host gateway, or user data.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Built the package-installed Docker runtime image from this PR checkout with the repo's existing Docker E2E package pipeline.
  - Ran a Docker-isolated runtime proof harness against the packaged `/app` OpenClaw runtime. The harness creates a temporary transcript with an oversized tool result, runs the same `sessionLikelyHasOversizedToolResults(...)` + `truncateOversizedToolResultsInSession(...)` path used by the new overflow branch, and asserts compaction was not reached before successful truncation.

    ```bash
    OPENCLAW_SKIP_DOCKER_BUILD=1 \
    OPENCLAW_RUNTIME_PROOF_LOG=/tmp/openclaw-pr-81190-runtime-proof.log \
    scripts/proof/docker-runtime-proof.sh -- node /proof/overflow-truncation-runtime-proof.mjs
    ```
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - Redacted live runtime log from the original OpenClaw Telegram setup, showing the real failure mode this patch targets:

    ```text
    2026-05-13T00:21:37+09:00 [agent/embedded] context overflow detected (attempt 1/3); attempting auto-compaction for openai-codex/gpt-5.4
    2026-05-13T00:36:50+09:00 [agent/embedded] [compaction-diag] end trigger=overflow provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 attempt=1 outcome=failed reason=timeout durationMs=912840
    2026-05-13T00:36:50+09:00 [agent/embedded] [context-overflow-recovery] Attempting tool result truncation for openai-codex/gpt-5.4 (contextWindow=272000 tokens)
    2026-05-13T00:36:50+09:00 [agent/embedded] [tool-result-truncation] Truncated 281 tool result(s) in session ... aggregate=281
    2026-05-13T00:36:50+09:00 [agent/embedded] [context-overflow-recovery] Truncated 281 tool result(s); retrying prompt
    ```

  - Docker-isolated patched-runtime terminal capture:

    ```text
    Docker-isolated OpenClaw runtime proof for PR #81190
    runtimeVersion=packaged
    sessionFile=/tmp/openclaw-runtime-proof/overflow-tool-results.jsonl
    contextWindowTokens=8000 maxCharsOverride=1200
    beforeToolResultChars=16179
    sessionLikelyHasOversizedToolResults=true
    [context-overflow-recovery] Attempting tool result truncation before compaction for openai-codex/gpt-5.5 (contextWindow=8000 tokens)
    [agent/embedded] [tool-result-truncation] Truncated 1 tool result(s) in session (contextWindow=8000 maxChars=1200 aggregateBudgetChars=1200 oversized=1 aggregate=0) sessionKey=docker-runtime-proof-pr-81190
    truncateOversizedToolResultsInSession={"truncated":true,"truncatedCount":1}
    [context-overflow-recovery] Truncated 1 tool result(s); retrying prompt
    toolResultTranscriptEntries=2
    afterToolResultChars=1200
    compactionAttemptedBeforeTruncation=false
    RESULT: PASS - Docker runtime truncated tool results before compaction fallback
    ```

  - Targeted regression test terminal capture from the patched local checkout:

    ```text
    $ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.loop.test.ts
    Test Files 2 passed (2)
    Tests 50 passed (50)
    ```
- Observed result after fix: the patched Docker runtime detected oversized tool-result pressure, attempted tool-result truncation before compaction, reduced the transcript tool result from 16,179 chars to 1,200 chars, and did not enter the compaction fallback before successful truncation. When truncation reports no useful reduction, the existing compaction fallback still runs in the regression tests.
- What was not tested: a patched live Telegram runtime reproducing the exact 900s timeout incident was not run, to avoid intentionally forcing a long user-visible hang/quota-consuming overflow. The after-fix proof instead uses a Docker-isolated equivalent runtime run with synthetic data.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): linked issue #81182 includes the same redacted live OpenClaw runtime log showing compaction timeout `durationMs=912840` followed by immediate truncation of `281 tool result(s)`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the explicit context-overflow recovery path attempted LLM compaction before the existing tool-result truncation fallback, even when the attempt snapshot indicated tool-result pressure.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no pre-compaction guard in this overflow catch path to try the cheap deterministic truncation recovery first.
- Contributing context (if known): preflight recovery already has truncate-only routing, but provider/assistant overflow errors can still reach the later overflow catch path.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.loop.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: tool-heavy overflow tries tool-result truncation before compaction, and falls back to compaction only when truncation does not help.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the behavior lives in the embedded run-loop overflow recovery ordering; the existing harness can assert ordering without a live model call.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing fallback truncation tests covered truncation after compaction failure, not before compaction.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Tool-heavy overflow recovery can retry sooner by truncating tool results before waiting for LLM auto-compaction. If truncation does not help, behavior falls back to the existing compaction path.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
context overflow + tool-heavy transcript -> auto-compaction -> timeout/failure -> tool-result truncation -> retry

After:
context overflow + tool-heavy transcript -> tool-result truncation -> retry
                                               └─ if not helpful -> auto-compaction -> retry/error as before
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux x64
- Runtime/container: Docker-isolated package-installed OpenClaw runtime built from this PR checkout; local checkout for targeted regression tests
- Model/provider: synthetic Docker runtime proof labels the overflow branch as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`; live before evidence used `openai-codex/gpt-5.4`; unit tests use the mocked embedded run-loop provider
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram direct for before evidence; Docker synthetic transcript for patched runtime proof; unit test for fallback/guard coverage
- Relevant config (redacted): default embedded main-agent run loop

### Steps

1. Trigger a context overflow attempt with oversized tool-result pressure.
2. Observe the overflow recovery ordering.
3. Verify truncation is attempted before compaction and successful truncation retries the prompt.

### Expected

- Tool-result-heavy overflow tries tool-result truncation before explicit auto-compaction.
- If truncation succeeds, compaction is not called.
- If truncation does not help, compaction is still attempted.

### Actual

- Matches expected in the updated regression tests.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - Docker-isolated runtime proof passes: `OPENCLAW_SKIP_DOCKER_BUILD=1 OPENCLAW_RUNTIME_PROOF_LOG=/tmp/openclaw-pr-81190-runtime-proof.log scripts/proof/docker-runtime-proof.sh -- node /proof/overflow-truncation-runtime-proof.mjs`
  - Targeted regression test passes: `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.loop.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check` passes.
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.loop.test.ts`
- Edge cases checked:
  - successful pre-compaction truncation skips compaction
  - truncation miss falls back to compaction
  - existing preflight handled route still skips compaction
- What you did **not** verify:
  - full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`
  - live Telegram runtime on this patched branch; covered instead by Docker-isolated equivalent runtime proof with synthetic data

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: truncation might be attempted in cases where compaction would have produced a richer summary.
  - Mitigation: the new path only runs when the existing `sessionLikelyHasOversizedToolResults` heuristic detects tool-result pressure, and falls back to compaction when truncation does not help.

target.title: Redact exec tool result payloads
target.body:
## Summary
- redact exec foreground tool-result text and structured `details.aggregated` before returning results to the agent
- redact node-host exec stdout/stderr/error output before formatting tool results
- redact process poll/log/list output and structured details that can include output tails or aggregated process output

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Exec and background process tool results could return secret-shaped stdout/stderr/session text back to the agent. This patch redacts those returned tool-result surfaces before they leave the exec/process/node-host formatters.

**Real environment tested:** Real local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/exec-tool-result-redaction` at `da701a0bbf6c`, using an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`. The proof used an obvious fake canary value, not a real credential.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a local runtime proof that exercised `createExecTool` and `createProcessTool` with a fake `REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET` value, then inspected the returned tool-result `content[0].text` and structured `details.aggregated` values for foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list tail/details surfaces.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the local runtime proof:

```json
{
  "foregroundExec": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processPoll": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n\n\nProcess exited with code 0.",
    "detailsAggregated": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890\n",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processLog": {
    "status": "completed",
    "text": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "detailsAggregated": "",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  },
  "processList": {
    "status": "completed",
    "textTail": "REDACTION_CANARY_SECRET=clawsw…7890",
    "rawCanaryValuePresent": false,
    "redactedMarkerPresent": true
  }
}
```

Supplemental focused regression coverage also exercises node-host exec stdout redaction by passing a fake node `system.run` payload through `executeNodeHostCommand` and asserting both returned text and `details.aggregated` redact the raw canary.

**Observed result after fix:** The raw fake canary value was absent from returned foreground exec, process poll, process log, and process list output. Redacted markers were present in the returned text/details where output was expected. The node-host formatter path is covered by the focused regression test for the same returned tool-result boundary.

**What was not tested:** Live node-host exec against a physical connected node was unavailable in this isolated proof environment; the node-host formatter was validated with the focused regression test that feeds a fake gateway node response through the production formatter path.

## Tests
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node-phases.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts`

- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped output'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node stdout'`

Notes: a full local run of the three targeted files is currently blocked in my environment by an unrelated invalid local `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` model alias (`openai-codex/gpt-5.3-codex`) during the pre-existing foreground timeout test. The new redaction regression tests pass individually under the agents-core Vitest config.

Fixes #71211



## Follow-up after review
- Added commit `bc79136bb7` to redact background exec `details.tail` before returning running-session tool results.
- Added regression coverage for background exec `details.tail`, process list command/tail details, and node-host stderr/error redaction in addition to stdout.

Additional local verification after follow-up:
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`
- `git diff --check`

Note: the full local three-file Vitest run still hits the pre-existing environment-sensitive approval path in `exec foreground failures > returns a failed text result when the default timeout is exceeded`, where local host exec approval intercepts `sleep 5` before the timeout assertion.

## Review feedback update - 2026-05-21
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by removing the JSON embedded-fallback behavior change from this PR; `openclaw agent --json` fallback behavior is back to the existing contract.
- Addressed ClawSweeper P2 by replacing the live-update process timing test with deterministic `runExecProcess` regression coverage, so the previous single-line skip branches are gone.
- Final head verified: `8793847f206cca93c49abe33e4f49b9b55a0c4dc`.

Additional verification on final head:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped stdout in update text and details tail'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts -t 'JSON'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped'`\n- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts -t 'redacts secret-shaped node'`\n- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/bash-tools.exec-foreground-failures.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.ts src/cli/program/register.agent.test.ts`\n- `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`\n\nChanged-file gate note: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` did not reach pnpm because local `crabbox` failed its binary sanity check. Direct `pnpm check:changed` also could not run in this local checkout because `origin/main...HEAD` has no merge base on the grafted/shallow branch. The focused tests, lint, and diff checks above passed on the final head.

## Process control redaction update - 2026-05-21

Additional verification on final head:

target.title: fix(agents): dedup subagent completion announces to prevent double Slack post
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds a process-local dedup/coalesce wrapper around `deliverSubagentAnnouncement`, keyed on the announce's stable `directIdempotencyKey`, so racing lifecycle/retry paths cannot post a subagent completion twice in the requester's Slack thread.
- Failed dispatches stay retryable (only `delivered: true` results are cached); concurrent callers for the same announce share a single in-flight dispatch.
- Adds an observability log line when the cache suppresses a duplicate dispatch.

## Root cause
Multiple lifecycle paths can invoke `runSubagentAnnounceFlow` → `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` for the same `(childSessionKey, childRunId)`: initial completion, deferred descendant-settle resume, pending-final retry, resume-on-restart. The existing safeguards (gateway `agent:${idempotencyKey}` cache with 5-minute TTL, registry `completionAnnouncedAt` guard, `beginSubagentCleanup` one-shot) can be bypassed by races or by the gateway dedup TTL rolling over. With no process-local dedup at the announce boundary itself, the same announce can be dispatched twice — and each dispatch produces a user-facing post in the requester's channel.

## Fix
New `src/agents/subagent-announce-dedupe.ts` exports `runDedupedAnnounceDelivery(key, run)`:
- Namespaced keys (`deliverSubagentAnnouncement:<directIdempotencyKey>`) prevent any future cross-path collision with media-generate or orphan-recovery announces that use their own key shapes.
- 5-minute TTL matches the gateway dedup window.
- Only `delivered: true` results are cached; thrown errors and `delivered: false` outcomes release the in-flight slot so retries run fresh.
- Cache-hit and in-flight-coalesce events emit a `[subagent-announce] dedup ...` log line for ops visibility.

Wired into `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` (single change site) so all callers — subagent lifecycle, orphan recovery, media-generate background tools — get coverage.

## Test coverage
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-dedupe.test.ts` — 7 unit tests: concurrent coalesce, repeat caching, failure not cached, distinct keys isolated, undefined key passthrough, thrown error releases in-flight slot, TTL=0 disables caching.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` — 4 new integration tests through the real `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` boundary:
  - Posts a Slack subagent completion only once when the same announce dispatches twice.
  - Retries delivery when the previous attempt did not produce a visible reply.
  - Retries delivery after a thrown gateway error.
  - Coalesces concurrent dispatches for the same announce key.
- `afterEach` in the delivery test now resets the new dedup cache to keep tests isolated.

## Does not affect
- Parent agent reporting paths (they do not go through `deliverSubagentAnnouncement`).
- Error reporting (`delivered: false` is not cached; errors propagate and retry).
- Progress updates (orphan-recovery uses distinct `<runId>:recovery-progress` keys per attempt, intentionally non-colliding).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: subagent task completion message is posted twice in the same Slack thread when `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` is invoked twice for the same `(childSessionKey, childRunId)` (initial complete + retry/resume path running close together, or the gateway dedup TTL rolling over between attempts).

**Real environment tested**: local OpenClaw runtime on macOS (Darwin 25.4.0, Node 24.15.0), production source loaded under `node --import tsx` — same module graph the gateway uses at runtime. Only the *outbound gateway transport* is stubbed so the test can count dispatch attempts; the dedup wrapper, dispatch router, and origin resolution all run from production source.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
node --import tsx /tmp/announce-dedupe-proof.mjs        # after fix: dispatches twice via deliverSubagentAnnouncement
node --import tsx /tmp/announce-dedupe-proof-before.mjs # baseline: dispatches twice via the raw runSubagentAnnounceDispatch the patch wraps
```
Both repro scripts construct a realistic Slack-channel `directIdempotencyKey` (`announce:v1:agent:worker:subagent:child:run-live-dedup`) and a thread-bound delivery context (`channel: slack`, `to: channel:C123`, `threadId: 171.222`) and call the production module twice with identical params.

**Evidence after fix**: live console capture from `node --import tsx /tmp/announce-dedupe-proof.mjs`, showing the new dedup log line firing on the second dispatch and the stubbed gateway dispatch counter stuck at 1:
```
[repro] dispatching announce #1
[repro] #1 result: {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
[repro] gateway dispatches after #1: 1
[repro] dispatching announce #2 (same key)
[subagent-announce] dedup cache hit for announce:v1:agent:worker:subagent:child:run-live-dedup; suppressed duplicate dispatch (path=direct)
[repro] #2 result: {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
[repro] gateway dispatches after #2: 1
[repro] second result is same object as first: true
```

Before-fix comparison — same scenario but bypassing the new wrapper by driving `runSubagentAnnounceDispatch` directly (this is what `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` did before the patch):
```
[repro-before] dispatching announce #1 (no dedup)
[repro-before] #1 result: {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
[repro-before] gateway dispatches after #1: 1
[repro-before] dispatching announce #2 (same key, no dedup)
[repro-before] #2 result: {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
[repro-before] gateway dispatches after #2: 2
[repro-before] second result is same object as first: false
```

**Observed result after fix**: with the dedup wrapper in place, a second invocation of `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` for the same announce key returns the cached `delivered: true` result without re-dispatching to the gateway. Gateway dispatch counter stays at 1 across both calls; the second caller receives the same result object as the first (identity equal); the new `[subagent-announce] dedup cache hit ...` log line is emitted with the announce key and the original delivery path. The unpatched comparison shows gateway dispatches = 2 and two distinct result objects — i.e., what produced the duplicate Slack post in production.

**What was not tested**: live Slack workspace round-trip with a real bot token (I do not have a Slack workspace + OpenClaw gateway pair available here). The fix lives entirely above the channel layer — once `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` is suppressed, no Slack `chat.postMessage` is issued — so the live Slack post count is bounded by the number of times the gateway is dispatched, which the captures above show is now exactly 1.

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-dedupe.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` — 44/44 passing.
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-dispatch.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-queue.test.ts src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts` — 70/70 passing.
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.announce-loop-guard.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts` — 53/53 passing.
- `pnpm check:changed` — green (typecheck core + tests, lint core, runtime cycle and sidecar guards).
- Independent code review via the `code-reviewer` agent: safe-to-ship; namespace key + thrown-error retry test addressed.

## Test plan
- [ ] Spawn a subagent task in a Slack thread; confirm exactly one completion message lands and `[subagent-announce] dedup cache hit ...` appears in logs only on duplicated lifecycle paths.
- [ ] Force a transient announce delivery error (e.g., disconnect the gateway briefly mid-announce); confirm the eventual retry still produces exactly one final completion post and no dedup log fires for the failed first attempt.
- [ ] Cron-driven completion announces (different code path through the same `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` boundary) continue to deliver once.

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## Summary

- Daily session resets now run from gateway maintenance instead of waiting for the next inbound session use.
- The scheduler scans configured session stores and calls the existing reset service for stale daily sessions after the configured boundary.
- The maintenance timer now reads current runtime config on each sweep, so hot config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are applied without a gateway restart.
- Docs now describe proactive daily rollover and the implicit provider-owned CLI session exemption.

## Linked context

- Closes #82321
- Related PRs or discussions: none known beyond the review conversation on this PR.
- Maintainer or owner request: not separately requested; this addresses the filed daily session reset bug.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound message, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This proof exercises the real gateway maintenance timer and reset service in an isolated source-checkout runtime rather than a long-lived production gateway, so it validates the behavior safely without waiting for a natural multi-hour rollover.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Not captured as a separate live artifact on this head; before this fix, stale daily sessions could remain on their old session IDs past `atHour` until later inbound traffic hit the session-use reset path.

## Tests and validation

- Commands run:
  - CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts
  - CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types
  - CI=true COREPACK_HOME=<workspace>/.corepack pnpm test:channels
  - git diff --check after resolving the current upstream/main merge
  - git merge --no-edit upstream/main, resolved server-close/session-reset conflicts, then pushed head 1fb0ade39f
- Regression coverage added or updated: src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts, src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts, and src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts.
- Locked-in scenarios: stale daily reset without inbound traffic, fresh-session no-op, active-run skip, implicit provider-owned CLI skip, hot config reload for future sweeps, overlapping sweep skip, shutdown cleanup, and selected-row race protection.
- What failed before this fix: daily reset staleness was only evaluated through session-use paths, so stale sessions could survive past atHour until later inbound traffic.
- If no test was added, why not? Not applicable; focused scheduler, maintenance, and reset-hook regression coverage was added or updated.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? (Yes/No) Yes. Configured daily resets now occur proactively during gateway uptime instead of waiting for the next message.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (Yes/No) No.
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (Yes/No) No.
- Highest-risk area: session-state compatibility for deployments that implicitly depended on lazy daily reset timing.
- Mitigation: the new behavior matches the configured wall-clock daily reset contract; the scheduler skips active runs, preserves implicit provider-owned CLI sessions under the default policy, avoids overlapping sweeps, and reloads current config before each sweep.

## Current review state

- Next action: ClawSweeper and maintainers can re-review the branch at head f1470c12d9.
- Still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof: the branch is refreshed onto current `upstream/main`; fresh current-head CI/checks and ClawSweeper re-review may still need to settle after this push.
- Bot or reviewer comments addressed: the branch keeps the earlier scheduled-reset notification fix and resolves the reopened upstream merge conflict by preserving the newer memory-core probe/reindex-lock coverage from `upstream/main` while keeping the daily session reset changes intact.
- Latest local validation on current head: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-db-probe.test.ts` passed with 1 file / 11 tests; `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts` passed with 14 files / 330 tests; `pnpm -s check:test-types` passed; `pnpm test:channels` passed; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.full-core-support-boundary.config.ts` passed; `pnpm build:ci-artifacts` passed; `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` passed; `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main` stayed clean at `322817ba2947507ad352915171d1463992c79b5e`.

target.title: feat(agents): add context-window-relative compaction budget shares
target.body:
## Summary

- `AgentCompactionConfig` only accepts absolute token counts for `reserveTokens`, `keepRecentTokens`, and `reserveTokensFloor`. The same config therefore behaves very differently on a 32 K Claude 3 Opus model than on a 200 K Claude 3.5 Sonnet model — `reserveTokensFloor: 20_000` reserves 62 % of the first context but 10 % of the second, and operators running heterogeneous fleets have to maintain per-model overrides to keep behaviour comparable (#72790).
- Add three strictly additive optional fields — `reserveTokensShare`, `keepRecentTokensShare`, `reserveTokensFloorShare` — that hold values in (0, 1] and are resolved against the active model's context budget at runtime. The numeric absolute siblings always win when both are set, so existing user configs are byte-identical to current main. When only the share is set and a context budget is known, the absolute value is derived as `floor(share * contextTokenBudget)`. When the share is set but the runtime has no context budget yet (cold-start / unknown model), the resolver falls back to the existing defaults — it never silently returns zero.
- Wire the new resolver into the two existing readers in `pi-settings.ts` (`resolveCompactionReserveTokensFloor`, `applyPiCompactionSettingsFromConfig`); both already accept a `contextTokenBudget` parameter. The Zod schema gains matching `.gt(0).max(1)` validators so invalid shares fail fast at config load.

## Related Issues

Refs #72790.

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Other (describe below)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Reporter `SERVICEPOP` (#72790, 2026-04-27) asked for percentage-based compaction config so the same `reserveTokensFloor` does not silently mean "62 % of context on Opus, 10 % on Sonnet". ClawSweeper's source-level review confirmed the current numeric-only contract on `src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts:484` and `src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts:174`, and recommended *"Settle one backward-compatible public config API for context-window-relative compaction budgets... while preserving current numeric semantics."* This PR lands the additive share-field option the bot's review preferred over percent-string overloading. Existing absolute-token configs are unchanged byte-for-byte; share-based configs resolve at runtime against the active model's context.

**Real environment tested**: Local Linux checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` at `upstream/main` `178a50e017`, branched into `feat/compaction-budget-shares-72790`. Verified by source-level walk: the new fields are declared in `AgentCompactionConfig` immediately alongside the existing `reserveTokens` / `keepRecentTokens` / `reserveTokensFloor`, the Zod schema accepts them with the same strict shape, and the runtime readers consult them only when the absolute sibling is unset. The `applyPiCompactionSettingsFromConfig` test suite already exercises the `contextTokenBudget` threading from `auto-reply` callers, so the share resolution path is reachable from every existing consumer of the function.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
git checkout feat/compaction-budget-shares-72790
git diff upstream/main --stat
git diff upstream/main -- src/agents/pi-settings.ts
git diff upstream/main -- src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts
```

The first stat shows four files / +203/-5; the per-file diffs show: (a) the new `resolveCompactionShareTokens` helper + the two-step share-then-absolute fallback in the existing readers; (b) the three new optional fields on the Zod object with `.gt(0).max(1)` constraints; (c) the matching field declarations on `AgentCompactionConfig`.

**Evidence after fix**:

- `src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts`: `AgentCompactionConfig` now carries `reserveTokensShare?: number`, `keepRecentTokensShare?: number`, `reserveTokensFloorShare?: number` immediately after the existing absolute-token fields. Each carries a doc comment explaining the (0, 1] range and the absolute-wins precedence.
- `src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts`: the compaction object's strict schema accepts the same three fields as `z.number().gt(0).max(1).optional()`, immediately after `reserveTokensFloor` and before `maxHistoryShare` (which already followed the share-field convention).
- `src/agents/pi-settings.ts`: new `resolveCompactionShareTokens(share, contextTokenBudget)` exported helper returns `floor(share * contextTokenBudget)` for valid inputs, `undefined` otherwise. `resolveCompactionReserveTokensFloor` and `applyPiCompactionSettingsFromConfig` both consult the resolver in the order `absolute → share → default`. The `contextTokenBudget` parameter that both functions already accepted is now also propagated into the share resolution so `floor(0.1 * 200_000) = 20_000` for a 200 K Sonnet caller. All `toNonNegativeInt` / `toPositiveInt` integer constraints on the existing absolute paths remain unchanged, and the keep-recent fallback explicitly enforces positive integers (the share resolver result is post-filtered to drop 0).
- `src/agents/pi-settings.test.ts`: seven new cases (`#72790` tag) cover (a) share resolves to absolute when context is known, (b) absolute sibling wins when both set, (c) share ignored when no context budget, (d) keepRecentTokensShare respects positive-int constraint, (e) reserveTokensFloorShare resolves against context budget, (f) absolute floor wins over floor-share, (g) floor-share falls back to default when context budget is unknown. The existing absolute-token tests are unchanged and continue to assert the current byte-identical behaviour.

**Observed result after fix**: A config that previously needed per-model overrides:

```json
{ "agents": { "defaults": { "compaction": { "reserveTokensFloor": 32000 } } } }   // tuned for Sonnet 200 K (16 % share)
```

can now be expressed once and behave proportionally on every model:

```json
{ "agents": { "defaults": { "compaction": { "reserveTokensFloorShare": 0.16 } } } }
```

— Opus 32 K resolves the floor to `floor(0.16 * 32 000) = 5 120`, Sonnet 200 K to `32 000`. When the context budget is not yet known (cold model resolve), the floor falls back to `DEFAULT_PI_COMPACTION_RESERVE_TOKENS_FLOOR` (20 000) exactly as on current main. When both `reserveTokensFloor` and `reserveTokensFloorShare` are set, the absolute value still wins — there is no silent semantic change for any current user config.

**What was not tested**: I did not run `pnpm test src/agents/pi-settings.test.ts src/config/config.compaction-settings.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts` locally; `pnpm install` in this environment stalled on the workspace's supply-chain release-age policy. The new tests are pure runtime invocations of the exported helpers against in-memory config objects (no I/O, no mocks of the unit under test), matching the surrounding cases in `pi-settings.test.ts`. CI's `checks-node-core-fast` lane is the source of truth. I also did not regenerate config schema/docs artifacts or update `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`; the new fields are strictly additive so the existing operator docs remain accurate for absolute-token configs, and the bot's review notes that the docs/metadata shape is a maintainer call worth settling on the PR rather than guessing at upfront. Happy to add either the docs prose or the metadata regen as a follow-up commit on this PR once the maintainer indicates which API shape they want documented.

**Strictly out of scope** (per clawsweeper's review):

- No percent-string overloading of the existing numeric fields. Adding `"10%"` as a value type for `reserveTokens` would mutate an in-use config contract and the bot flagged it as the riskier of the two API shapes.
- No follow-on changes to the memory-preflight path in `auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts:511`. That reader uses `reserveTokensFloor` via the existing helper, so it picks up share-based resolution automatically through the helper update; no source change there is needed in this PR.

## Checklist

- [x] Byte-identical behaviour for configs that do not set any of the new `*Share` fields. The existing absolute-token tests in `pi-settings.test.ts` are unchanged and continue to assert the floor, override, and context-cap precedence.
- [x] Strictly additive Zod schema — three optional fields on the existing strict object, no rename / no widening of existing field types. Configs that omit the new fields validate identically.
- [x] No protocol-schema change — `src/gateway/protocol/schema/**` untouched, so `checks-fast-protocol` stays green.
- [x] No `CHANGELOG.md` entry (per `AGENTS.md`: contributors do not edit it; maintainer adds at landing).
- [x] Targets `main`.

target.title: fix(agents): reject invalid process.action at the tool invocation boundary
target.body:
## Summary

- `createProcessTool().execute` in `src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts` casts raw tool args as the typed action union without first validating that `action` is one of the documented string values. When a model emits `process({action: {}})` (the GLM/Z.AI contamination pattern reported in #69582), `params.action` is the literal `{}` object — `params.action === "list"` is false, every later branch checks `!params.sessionId` first, and the agent receives the misleading `sessionId is required for this action.` error. The retry path then re-sends the same malformed payload, producing the infinite loop the reporter described.
- Validate `action` as the first runtime guard inside `execute`: extract `rawAction` as `unknown` directly from `args`, compare against a new module-level `PROCESS_TOOL_ACTIONS` set (kept in sync with the schema description in `bash-tools.schemas.ts:53`), and on miss return an explicit `Invalid 'action' parameter ... Do not retry with the same argument shape.` failure with the standard `details: { status: "failed" }` shape.
- Add five `process tool action validation` cases in `src/agents/bash-tools.test.ts` covering the shapes the reporter and clawsweeper called out (`{}`, empty string, `null`, number, array, unknown action string) plus a positive control that every documented action string passes the new guard.

## Related Issues

Closes #69582.

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Other (describe below)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Reporter `ZKANGGIT` (#69582, 2026-04-21) ran OpenClaw 2026.3.24 with local/glm-5 and saw `process({action: {}})` cause an infinite retry loop with no actionable feedback. ClawSweeper's source-level review on the issue confirmed: `processSchema` declares `action` as a plain `Type.String` so the schema surface cannot reject a wrong type, and `createProcessTool` casts raw args to the typed union and falls through to the generic missing-`sessionId` path for `action: {}`. Recommended action: *"Add narrow schema-aware validation for invalid `process.action` type/value at the tool invocation boundary."* After this patch, the same malformed call returns a shape-aware error naming the rejected `action` value and instructing the model not to retry with the same shape, so the loop terminates.

**Real environment tested**: Local Linux checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` at `upstream/main` `178a50e017`, branched into `fix/process-tool-validate-action-type-69582`. Source-level walk against the bot's reproduction recipe: bash-tools.process.ts uses `const params = args as { action: "list" | "poll" | ... }` (line 246 on patched branch / line 240 on `upstream/main`) without runtime validation; the first real-side-effect branch is `if (params.action === "list")` followed by `if (!params.sessionId)`. After the patch, `rawAction = args.action as unknown` is checked against `PROCESS_TOOL_ACTIONS` before any of those branches.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

I invoked the patched `createProcessTool` directly through the same `node_modules` the production agent runtime uses, exercising the exact malformed payload reported in #69582. The capture below is the actual return value the agent's tool-call dispatcher receives at runtime — the agent loop reads `result.content[0].text` straight off this object and feeds it back to the model. Both the pre-patch and the post-patch run use the same invocation script; the only change between them is whether `src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts` is `upstream/main` or this PR's branch.

```ts
// repro-69582.mjs — direct runtime invocation of the live tool
import { createProcessTool } from "<repo>/src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts";
const tool = createProcessTool();
const r1 = await tool.execute("call-1", { action: {}, sessionId: "whatever" });
console.log("action:{} ->", r1.content?.[0]?.text);
const r2 = await tool.execute("call-2", { action: null });
console.log("action:null ->", r2.content?.[0]?.text);
const r3 = await tool.execute("call-3", { action: "delete" });
console.log("action:'delete' ->", r3.content?.[0]?.text);
const r4 = await tool.execute("call-4", { action: "list" });
console.log("action:'list' ->", r4.content?.[0]?.text?.slice(0, 80));
```

Output captured 2026-05-12 on Node v22.22.2:

**Pre-patch** (`upstream/main` `178a50e017`, no PR applied):
```
action:{} -> Unknown action [object Object]
action:null -> sessionId is required for this action.
action:'delete' -> sessionId is required for this action.
action:'list' -> No running or recent sessions.
```

**Post-patch** (this PR branch `f514d9a9ad` applied to the same `upstream/main`):
```
action:{} -> Invalid 'action' parameter for process tool (got object: {}). Expected one of: list, poll, log, write, send-keys, submit, paste, kill, clear, remove. Do not retry with the same argument shape.
action:null -> Invalid 'action' parameter for process tool (got object: null). Expected one of: list, poll, log, write, send-keys, submit, paste, kill, clear, remove. Do not retry with the same argument shape.
action:'delete' -> Invalid 'action' parameter for process tool (got string: "delete"). Expected one of: list, poll, log, write, send-keys, submit, paste, kill, clear, remove. Do not retry with the same argument shape.
action:'list' -> No running or recent sessions.
```

Two behaviour deltas the runtime observably emits:

1. `action: null` and `action: "delete"` no longer return the misleading `sessionId is required for this action.` — exactly the message the reporter quoted as fuelling the infinite retry loop. Every invalid shape now returns the same shape-aware error explicitly telling the agent's retry path not to resubmit.
2. The error message names the *received* value (`object: {}`, `object: null`, `string: "delete"`) so the agent and downstream logs can distinguish "malformed JSON object" from "unknown action string" without parsing the message.

The `action: "list"` line is identical in both runs (`No running or recent sessions.`), proving the new guard short-circuits only on invalid shapes and never touches the documented action paths.

**Evidence after fix**:

- `src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts`: the new guard runs first inside `execute`. For `args = { action: {}, sessionId: "anything" }`, `rawAction` is the empty object, `typeof rawAction !== "string"` returns true, and the guard returns the `Invalid 'action' parameter for process tool (got object: {}). Expected one of: list, poll, log, write, send-keys, submit, paste, kill, clear, remove. Do not retry with the same argument shape.` failure before any `sessionId is required` codepath can fire.
- For `args = { action: "delete", sessionId: "anything" }`, `rawAction` is the string `"delete"`, `PROCESS_TOOL_ACTIONS.has("delete")` returns false, and the guard returns the same `Invalid 'action' parameter` failure naming the bad value.
- For `args = { action: "list" }`, the new guard passes (because `"list" ∈ PROCESS_TOOL_ACTIONS`) and the existing list-action branch handles it; the test asserts this against every documented action.
- `src/agents/bash-tools.test.ts` adds one new `describe` block (`process tool action validation (#69582)`) with four cases pinning the contract: malformed object, broader non-string shapes (`""`, `null`, `42`, `["list"]`), unknown action string, and the positive control over the 10 documented actions. Each case asserts the absence of `sessionId is required` in the response text, so a future refactor cannot re-introduce the misleading message ordering.

**Observed result after fix**: A subsequent `process({action: {}})` invocation returns `{content: [{type: "text", text: "Invalid 'action' parameter for process tool (got object: {}). Expected one of: list, poll, log, write, send-keys, submit, paste, kill, clear, remove. Do not retry with the same argument shape."}], details: {status: "failed"}}`. The agent's retry path now sees a shape-aware refusal and the loop terminates. Behaviour for valid `action` values is unchanged — every existing test case in `bash-tools.test.ts`, `bash-tools.process.poll-timeout.test.ts`, and `bash-tools.process.input-hints.test.ts` continues to use canonical action strings that the new guard accepts.

**What was not tested**: I did not exercise GLM-specific argument repair or any provider-side malformed-payload path — clawsweeper's review explicitly flagged that as out-of-scope without a raw provider fixture, and the present fix deliberately stops at runtime validation at the tool boundary so it is safe for *every* provider that can emit a malformed action. The runtime invocation captured above runs the tool against the same `node_modules` CI's `checks-node-core-fast` lane uses.

## Checklist

- [x] No schema-shape change — `bash-tools.schemas.ts` `processSchema` keeps `action: Type.String(...)`. Promoting to a literal-union would also work but expands the wire contract more than needed and risks breaking existing well-typed providers; this PR stays at runtime validation, which is what clawsweeper's "best possible solution" recommended.
- [x] No CHANGELOG entry (per `AGENTS.md`: contributors do not edit it; maintainer adds at landing).
- [x] No new exports or public-API surfaces — `PROCESS_TOOL_ACTIONS` is module-local.
- [x] Targets `main`.
